channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Daniil Medvedev destroys a TV camera attached to the net during his 5-set Australian Open win
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Daniil Medvedev used his racket to smash a tiny camera attached to the net at the Australian Open while he was trailing someone ranked 418th before eventually avoiding a monumental upset and winning 6-2, 4-6, 3-6, 6-1, 6-2 in the first round at Rod Laver Arena on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Former Padres Peavy, Gonzalez headline 77th Salute to the Champions
item.description: The Padres duo, along with WNBA star Charde Houston, now have a permanent place inside the Western Metal Supply Company building at Petco Park as Breitbard Hall of Famers.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Stone, Fox 5

item.title: Jenson Brooksby returns to pro tennis at the Australian Open after 2 eventful years away
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Two eventful years after his most recent official tennis match — a stretch that included a pair of wrist operations, a bum shoulder, a coaching change and a suspension related to missed drug tests — Jenson Brooksby returned to action Tuesday at the Australian Open.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Lakers lose to Spurs 126-102 in their 1st game back after wildfire postponements
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Victor Wembanyama had 23 points and eight rebounds, Harrison Barnes scored 10 of his 17 points in the fourth quarter, and the San Antonio Spurs beat the Lakers 126-102 on Monday night in Los Angeles' first game back from two postponements due to the wildfires devastating the area.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sam Darnold's career-best season ends with a thud as he takes 9 sacks in playoff loss
item.description: GLENDALE, Ariz. (AP) — Sam Darnold did nearly everything right in a renaissance season, resurrecting his career as a starter while pushing Minnesota to one of the NFL's best records. A deep playoff run was the expected next step.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MARSHALL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Patrick Fishburn nearly wins first PGA Tour event
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Former BYU star Patrick Fishburn had visions of winning his first PGA Tour event. He was leading after two rounds, and one shot back after three rounds before ending up tied for sixth.
item.dc:creator: Wesley Ruff, ABC 4

item.title: Lakers retire Michael Cooper's No. 21 on an emotional night for the Hall of Fame guard from Pasadena
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Michael Cooper spent his entire career with the Los Angeles Lakers as a defense-minded guard who always focused on helping his Showtime teammates to be stars. When the Lakers raised Cooper's No.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Rams overcome chaotic week to rout Vikings in playoff game
item.description: The Los Angeles Rams had a lot on their mind as they prepared for the Minnesota Vikings. But the defense held strong in the team's wild-card win.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Novak Djokovic and Andy Murray chatted mid-match thanks to the new Australian Open coaches' box
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Novak Djokovic stood with his hands on his hips, listening intently to Andy Murray — his longtime on-court rival and now, stunningly, his coach at the Australian Open — between sets at Rod Laver Arena. Murray pantomimed the swing of a two-handed backhand.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Lakers, Clippers return to their home courts after days of fire devastation across Los Angeles
item.description: The Lakers and the Clippers both went back to work Monday night in Los Angeles’ first two NBA games since catastrophic wildfires killed at least 24 people and destroyed significant sections of their hometown.
item.dc:creator: Greg Beacham, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Eagles fan directs vile tirade at Packers fan during wild-card game, social media video shows
item.description: Philadelphia Eagles fans are known as some of the most passionate supporters in the NFL, but one fan's behavior during a playoff game is raising eyebrows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cubs sign former Brewers starting pitcher to one-year deal
item.description: The Chicago Cubs have signed former Milwaukee Brewers starting pitcher Colin Rea to a new contract for the 2025 MLB season, while the Cubs have designated RHP Michael Arias for assignment to make room for Rea on the 40-man roster.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Texas man charged with stalking WNBA and Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark
item.description: Authorities in Indianapolis have charged a 55-year-old Texas man with felony stalking of Indiana Fever star and WNBA rookie of the year Caitlin Clark. Michael Thomas Lewis is accused of repeated and continued harassment of the 22-year-old Clark beginning on Dec.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Patrick, Brittany Mahomes announce birth of 3rd child
item.description: Patrick and Brittany Mahomes on Monday announced the birth of their third child. The NFL power couple named their child Golden Raye Mahomes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mike McCarthy won't return as Dallas Cowboys coach, AP source says
item.description: Mike McCarthy is not returning as coach of the Dallas Cowboys, who are going on three decades since their last Super Bowl title, a person with knowledge of the decision told The Associated Press on Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota earns 1st women's AP Top 25 ranking since 2019. UCLA, South Carolina, Notre Dame still 1-3
item.description: Minnesota is off to its best start in 17 games and earned its first ranking since 2019 on Monday, entering The Associated Press women's basketball poll at No. 24. The Golden Gophers have won 16 of their first 17 contests, with the loss coming against Nebraska last month.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-Florida QB Jesse Palmer talks College Football Playoff, French-onion beer, and Shedeur Sanders, Cam Ward
item.description: Jesse Palmer recently sat down with Fox News Digital to discuss the College Football Playoff, a new partnership, and Shedeur Sanders and Cam Ward.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Coaching Tracker: The Bears' search to find their next head coach
item.description: The Chicago Bears have assembled a six-person panel to find their next head coach, with Ryan Poles as the final decision-maker, and have requested to interview Joe Brady, Thomas Brown, Matt Campbell, Pete Carroll, Brian Flores, Marcus Freeman, Aaron Glenn, Joe Brady, and David Shaw.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: New England hires former Patriots Super Bowl champ Mike Vrabel as coach
item.description: The New England Patriots have hired Mike Vrabel as their head coach. The team announced the hiring Sunday morning, a week after Patriots owner Robert Kraft fired Jerod Mayo following the team’s season-ending victory over the Buffalo Bills. Mayo finished 4-13 in his lone season.
item.dc:creator: KYLE HIGHTOWER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Clippers, Lakers to resume their wildfire-delayed schedules on Monday
item.description: NBA games are scheduled to return to wildfire-ravaged Los Angeles on Monday night, with the Clippers planning to resume their schedule when they host the Miami Heat and the Lakers set to host Victor Wembanyama and the San Antonio Spurs.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New England Patriots hire former Super Bowl champ Mike Vrabel as next coach
item.description: Vrabel, a fan favorite during eight seasons as a player in New England where he was a member of its first three Super Bowl winners and an inductee into the team’s Hall of Fame in 2023, was the preferred candidate because of his long association with the franchise and coaching success.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Report: Bears will interview Ron Rivera for head coaching position; team says it has interviewed Lions coordinators
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. — The Bears will reportedly consider a man tied to both of their Super Bowl appearances to try to lead them back there one day. The Chicago Tribune reports the Bears are expected to interview Ron Rivera on Sunday, according to NFL sources.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Utah HC loses on late goal to Islanders, 2-1
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Mathew Barzal scored with 1:25 left in regulation and the New York Islanders defeated the Utah Hockey Club 2-1 at Delta Center on Saturday. The forward found a rebound off a scrum in front of the net and lifted it over a sprawling Connor Ingram from the right circle.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Devin Booker scores 34 in Suns' 114-106 win over Jazz
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) - Devin Booker scored 34 points and the Phoenix Suns never trailed in a 114-106 victory over the Utah Jazz on Saturday. Booker had 22 points in the first half on 7-of-8 shooting, including 4 of 5 from 3-point range. He finished 12 for 20 overall and 6 for 12 from behind the arc.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Nationals and Jorge López agree to $3 million, 1-year deal
item.description: The Washington Nationals and former All-Star pitcher Jorge López agreed to terms on a 1-year contract, the team announced Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Single-match tickets for San Diego FC games now available
item.description: The 2025 inaugural season for San Diego FC is just around the corner and fans can now purchase single-match tickets for all 21 home games.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, Fox 5

item.title: Chicago faces Sacramento, aims for 5th straight home win
item.description: Sacramento Kings (19-19, ninth in the Western Conference) vs. Chicago Bulls (18-20, 10th in the Eastern Conference) Chicago; Sunday, 3:30 p.m. EST BOTTOM LINE: Chicago hosts Sacramento aiming to extend its four-game home winning streak. The Bulls have gone 8-11 at home.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Lindsey Vonn impresses skiing into 6th place in her first World Cup downhill race since 2019
item.description: The racecourse commentator broadcasting Lindsey Vonn's first World Cup downhill race since 2019 — the American is age 40 and has a titanium knee — summed up how impressive the ski great's performance was.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ohio State getting shot at 6th national title after 28-14 win over Texas in CFP semi Cotton Bowl
item.description: Sawyer stripped Texas quarterback Quinn Ewers — his former roommate — and returned the fumble 83 yards for the clinching touchdown in a 28-14 victory over the Longhorns in the Cotton Bowl on Friday night, giving the Buckeyes a shot at their sixth AP national title.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Red Wings hold off Blackhawks 5-3 for 6th straight victory
item.description: Alex DeBrincat and Lucas Raymond each had a goal and an assist and the Detroit Red Wings won their sixth straight game, beating the Chicago Blackhawks 5-3 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: LA Rams head for Arizona and a crucial playoff game with their minds on their fire-ravaged hometown
item.description: The Los Angeles Rams wrapped up practice at their training complex Friday under blue skies with bruise-colored clouds of wildfire smoke lurking on the horizon.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Real Salt Lake trades leading scorer Chicho Arango
item.description: In the span of one year, Real Salt Lake's high-powered offense has been dismantled, leaving some fans questioning the team's direction and competitive outlook.
item.dc:creator: Mattey Casey, ABC 4

item.title: CMU Basketball: Men get first RMAC win under Dunlap as Women cruise past NMHU
item.description: After a three-week break, Colorado Mesa Basketball is back. And they're off to a great start in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Google Trends: Nearly every US state is searching the Broncos over the Bills
item.description: We looked at Google Trends statistics to see whether the Broncos or the Bills were the talk of the country – and one team won by a landslide.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, Fox 5

item.title: Jazz lose on half-court shot at the buzzer
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Jazz' attempt at its first three-game winning streak of the season ended in heartbreaking fashion. Trae Young hit a buzzer-beating 3-pointer from beyond the halfcourt line to give the Atlanta Hawks a 124-121 victory over the Jazz on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Aggies avoid upset at San Jose State, 85-78
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (ABC4 Sports) - In their first game of the season as a ranked team, the Utah State basketball team avoided a major upset at San Jose State Tuesday night. Mason Falslev had 17 points and seven rebounds, Tucker Anderson added 15 points and No.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Peter Seidler's brother responds to lawsuit over control of Padres
item.description: Matt Seidler, the brother of late Padres owner Peter Seidler, has responded to the lawsuit filed by Peter Seidler's widow over control of the team.
item.dc:creator: Troy Hirsch, Fox 5
